title: NRC unveils Part 53 final rule
claim_supported: NRC finalized 10 CFR Part 53, a technology-inclusive advanced-reactor licensing framework, unlocking advanced-reactor BOP procurement.
locator: Article body, rule-approval and framework-summary paragraphs, quoting NRC Chairman Ho Nieh.
notes: Direct fetch of federalregister.gov and nrc.gov PDF was blocked/timed out this session; effective date (2026-04-29, 30 days after 2026-03-30 FR publication) is corroborated by multiple law-firm trackers (Orrick, BDLaw, Morgan Lewis) found via search but not independently fetched. Classified trade_press (T2) rather than government (T1) because the .gov source itself was not successfully fetched this session.

title: China's Advanced Nuclear Efforts Are Pushing Frontiers
claim_supported: CNNC plans a molten-salt energy storage plus sCO2 generation pilot, a selected Chinese national first-of-a-kind demonstration project, envisioning completion in 2028.
locator: Article body, direct quote: "CNNC now reportedly plans to scale up applications through a molten-salt energy storage plus sCO2 generation pilot -- as a selected Chinese national 'first-of-a-kind' demonstration project -- envisioning completion in 2028."
notes: This is a genuinely distinct project from Chaotan One (Guizhou, already operational). The longlist record's demand_trigger_2030_2034 field cites atlas L04-051 for a 'CNNC Xinjiang molten-salt+sCO2 2028' claim, but L04-051 is the CCTV article about Chaotan One (Guizhou) -- a citation mismatch. Xinjiang location for this distinct project is corroborated only via search-level Chinese-language aggregation this session, not independently fetched.

india_origin_audit.notes: US-based energy trade publication; subject matter is CNNC (China) but reporting organization is US-domiciled.

title: 全球首套超临界二氧化碳余热发电项目全面建成，"超碳一号"第二台机组并网 (World's first commercial sCO2 waste-heat power project fully built; Super Carbon One second unit grid-connected)
authors_or_org: Sina Technology (新浪科技), reporting on CNNC/Nuclear Power Institute of China Chaotan One project
claim_supported: Chaotan One's second sCO2 unit grid-connected 2026-05-30 at Shougang Shuicheng steel facility, bringing the project to 30MW stable installed capacity and ~230 million kWh/year.
locator: Article body, grid-connection date and installed-capacity paragraphs, dated 2026-05-31.
notes: Refreshes/updates atlas L04-048/051 (first-unit-only status) with confirmed full-project (both units) operational status as of session date.

institutions.name: Sina Technology / CNNC Nuclear Power Institute of China

india_origin_audit.notes: CN-domiciled outlet and CN state-owned project developer; no India affiliation.

title: VPE Products -- Vacuum Process Engineering
claim_supported: VPE (Sacramento, CA) manufactures diffusion-bonded microchannel/printed-circuit heat exchangers for sCO2 power cycles, with a recent hybrid dry-cooler build for sCO2; serves defense/government/laser customers.
locator: Product page body, MCHE/PCHE description and recent-builds sections.
notes: Fills the seed's own flagged 'not in atlas -- flag for P4' PCHE-competitor gap. US-domiciled, DOE-linked merchant competitor to Heatric.

india_origin_audit.notes: US-domiciled company, Sacramento, California address on company site.

title: Four China Nuclear Industry Companies Added to "Entity List"
claim_supported: Names the four China General Nuclear (CGN)-affiliated entities added to the BIS Entity List on 2019-08-14 (CGN, CGNPC, China Nuclear Power Technology Research Institute, Suzhou Nuclear Power Research Institute) with presumption-of-denial license policy.
locator: Article body, listed-entities and license-policy paragraphs.
notes: Law-firm summary of the underlying Federal Register action (2019-08-14); federalregister.gov itself could not be directly fetched this session due to a redirect block. Used to establish that CGN Dasheng Electron Accelerator Technology (the seed's named buyer) is NOT one of the four explicitly listed entities, though it is an admitted subsidiary of the named parent group -- a counterparty-diligence ambiguity, not a confirmed listing.

title: The North American Facility Gap: A 2025 Update
claim_supported: US sterilization-facility count needed to grow from ~150 (2022) to ~240 (2025 projected), an ~18-facility shortfall; e-beam facility count doubled 2022-2025 amid EtO closures.
locator: Article body, facility-count and EO-closure-impact sections.
rejection_reason: P4 context citation; claim co-supported by primary sources; retained as working evidence, not a canonical atlas member
notes: Demoted: NextBeam is an e-beam-adjacent engineering/advisory firm; this is vendor-adjacent market analysis, not independent customer evidence -- the evidence file itself labels it 'used here only as directional market-structure context' and cross-checks it against independently-confirmed Steris/Sotera duopoly share data, meaning it is contextual color rather than sole support for a load-bearing claim. Retained as working evidence, not promoted to the canonical atlas.

title: ScandiNova Celebrates Major Achievement: 3000 New-Generation Pulse Modulators Delivered
claim_supported: CORRECTED after direct WebFetch 2026-07-13: ScandiNova (now part of Nodica Group) has delivered its 3,000th new-generation pulse modulator as a cumulative milestone. The press release itself states ScandiNova has 'over 200 customers in more than 50 countries' -- it does NOT state '150 customers and 2,000+ modulators installed historically' as previously recorded; that specific figure is not supported by this source and should not be repeated. ScandiNova remains the clearest named incumbent in the pulsed-power-modulator space based on the confirmed 3,000-unit/200-customer figures.
locator: Direct WebFetch of prnewswire.com release, 2026-07-13: headline (3,000th unit delivered) and 'over 200 customers in more than 50 countries' sentence.
notes: Content corrected to match the actually-fetched press release text (200+ customers/50+ countries, 3,000th-unit milestone); the previously-recorded '150 customers/2,000+ modulators' figure was not found in this document and has been removed.

institutions.name: ScandiNova Systems AB / Nodica Group AB

india_origin_audit.notes: Sweden-headquartered pulsed-power company distributing via PR Newswire (US wire service).

title: Commonwealth Fusion Systems installs reactor magnet, lands deal with Nvidia
claim_supported: CFS's SPARC toroidal-field magnets operate at -253C (20K); all 18 magnets expected complete by end of summer 2026; reactor turn-on/first plasma targeted 2027.
locator: Article body, magnet-specification and installation-timeline paragraphs, dated 2026-01-06.
notes: Direct, concrete match to the seed's 20-80K target temperature band from a named, funded US fusion company; SPARC's own cryoplant was already procured in an earlier funding round (search-confirmed), so this is demand-class evidence (future CFS-class buyers), not an open near-term RFP from CFS itself.

title: US Entity list update, 7 Chinese companies added in relation to quantum
claim_supported: BIS added Scikro Instrument (Shanghai/Hong Kong) and Physike Technology (Beijing) to the Entity List in March 2025 specifically for supplying dilution refrigerators to Entity-listed/defense parties in China.
notes: Confirms the seed's flagged '2025 Entity-List additions targeted Chinese cryo suppliers' claim is real but product-specific to dilution refrigerators (mK/quantum-qubit cooling), not directly to the seed's 20-80K industrial/fusion band -- narrows rather than confirms the seed's own framing.

india_origin_audit.notes: Independent US/Western-run quantum-industry newsletter tracking BIS actions; no India affiliation identified.

title: 国产"超级低温工厂"攻坚纪实----科技自立自强之路 (Record of China's domestic 'super cryogenic factory' breakthrough)
authors_or_org: Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) / Institute of Physics (CAS-IOP/IET)
claim_supported: CAS Institute of Physics developed gas-bearing helium turbo-expander cryoplants incl. a 2kW@20K-class system, with 18 units deployed domestically and internationally including South Korea's KSTAR fusion facility; spun off Beijing Zhongke Fuhai Cryogenic Technology Co. in 2016.
locator: Article body, technical-specification and commercialization/spinoff-company sections.
notes: Competitor-context only, not a CN-market demand claim for this idea (idea is US-only per binding downgrade). Shows the underlying gas-bearing turbo-expander technology is not Western-exclusive; China has an internationally-exported capability in an overlapping power/temperature class. TIER UPGRADE (P4 curation): retiered T2->T1 -- this is an official Chinese Academy of Sciences primary institutional record (cas.cn, the national academy's own domain) describing CAS-IOP/IET's own technology and its commercialization spinoff, meeting the T1 definition of a government/national-lab primary record.

india_origin_audit.notes: Official PRC state-academy domain (cas.cn); no India affiliation.

title: SHI Cryogenics Group -- Advanced Cryogenic Technology Solutions
authors_or_org: Sumitomo (SHI) Cryogenics of America, Inc. (Sumitomo Heavy Industries, Ltd.)
claim_supported: Sumitomo's single-stage GM cryocooler line spans 20K-77K, newest model (CH-160D3) rated 100W@20K and 725W@77K, serving medical/semiconductor/R&D markets.
notes: Competitor evidence for the binding P4 competitor-map requirement -- GM-cycle (not turbo-Brayton), sub-seed power class, but directly adjacent, satisfying the 'Stirling/GM vendors' mapping requirement.

india_origin_audit.notes: US subsidiary of Japan-headquartered parent; no India affiliation.

title: Turbo-Brayton Technology (product profile)
authors_or_org: Air Liquide Advanced Technologies, via BiogasWorld
claim_supported: Air Liquide's Turbo-Brayton line is a commercially available product today, covering 25K-200K at up to 70 bar, used for HTS-cable cooling among other applications, with a design (all active elements on one shaft) directly analogous to the seed's gas-bearing turboalternator approach.
locator: Product page body, technology-description and temperature/pressure-range sections.
notes: The single most important competitor finding for C-12: materially weakens the 'mid-band gap' framing for the 25-80K portion of the seed's target band. Satisfies the binding P4 competitor-map requirement together with S03/S04.

india_origin_audit.notes: France-headquartered multinational industrial gas company; BiogasWorld (Canada-based directory) hosts the product profile.

title: Commerce Implements Export Controls on Semiconductor, Additive Manufacturing, and Quantum Computing Items
claim_supported: Confirms ECCN 3A904/3E901 ('cryogenic cooling systems and specified components, and technology') was added to the Commerce Control List effective 2024-09-06.
notes: Confirms the ECCN exists and its effective date; does not itself state the 0.1K/600 microwatt numeric threshold (that figure is corroborated across multiple other law-firm summaries found via search this session, not independently fetched in full text). Federal Register direct fetch was blocked this session.

title: Wuhan Raycus Fiber Laser Technologies Co., Ltd. -- Sanctions Summary
authors_or_org: Sanctions Finder (aggregating US BIS Entity List data)
claim_supported: Wuhan Raycus Fiber Laser Technologies Co., Ltd. was added to the BIS Entity List on 2021-07-12, with a presumption-of-denial license policy applying to all EAR-controlled exports/reexports/transfers.
locator: Summary record body: listing date, program/list, license requirement, and license policy fields.
notes: Critical finding: Raycus is the seed's own named CN buyer example for P3R2-C-13. This is a secondary aggregator of the underlying BIS/Federal Register action (federalregister.gov direct fetch was blocked this session); corroborated independently by P3R2-C-13-S02 (Chinese-language trade press) with matching date and reason.

india_origin_audit.notes: US BIS-data aggregation service; underlying regulator is US Department of Commerce.

title: 最新消息！锐科激光被美国列入实体清单！ (Breaking news: Raycus added to the US Entity List!)
claim_supported: Confirms Raycus was added to the BIS Entity List on 2021-07-09, among 34 entities added (22 Chinese), citing unauthorized-military-end-use procurement risk; reports Raycus 2020 revenue RMB2.317bn (+15.25% YoY) as pre-listing context.
locator: Article body, listing-date and company-revenue paragraphs.
notes: CN-language independent corroboration of P3R2-C-13-S01's Entity List finding, satisfying the mandatory zh-query requirement for this CN-leg finding.

title: nLight wins $44M laser weapon contract from Defense Dept
claim_supported: nLIGHT awarded a Joint Laser Weapon System (JLWS) OTA agreement from the US DoD, $44M initial award, up to $627M total program ceiling, announced 2026-07-09, targeting cruise-missile defense with operational demonstrations planned as early as 2028.
locator: Article body, contract-value, awarding-agency, and program-timeline paragraphs.
notes: Refines the seed's cited $847M JLWS ceiling figure to a directly-fetched $627M figure -- reconcile against original atlas source L12-031/032 before final scoring; both describe large, real DoD-committed DEW funding.

india_origin_audit.notes: US-domiciled company and US federal awarding agency.

title: nLight delivers record sales as laser weapon demand intensifies
claim_supported: nLIGHT FY2025 aerospace & defense revenue reached $175M (+60% YoY) of a record $261M total revenue; named programs HELSI-2 (megawatt-scale missile defense) and DE M-SHORAD (50kW, delivered to US Army).
locator: Article body, FY2025 results and named-program paragraphs.
notes: Reports on nLIGHT's own company-filing figures; primary US directed-energy demand evidence.

india_origin_audit.notes: UK-based photonics trade-press outlet (SPIE Europe) reporting on a US company.

title: 锐科激光一季度营收8.59亿元同比增20.06% (Raycus Q1 revenue RMB859M, +20.06% YoY)
authors_or_org: Sina Finance (AI-assisted earnings summary), reporting on Wuhan Raycus Fiber Laser Technologies Co., Ltd. Q1 2026 report
claim_supported: Raycus Q1 2026 revenue RMB859M (+20.06% YoY), net profit RMB41.8M (+147.04% YoY), gross margin 22.05% (+3.16pp YoY), sales expenses +70.34% YoY.
notes: Refreshes the seed's cited 2025 price-war-compressed figures (L12-037) with a 2026 rebound data point; note the article's revenue figure was extracted by an automated summary as '8.59 billion yuan,' which is a unit error -- the correct figure per the article's own Chinese text (8.59亿元) is RMB859 million, used here.

institutions.name: Wuhan Raycus Fiber Laser Technologies Co., Ltd.

india_origin_audit.notes: CN-domiciled outlet and CN-listed (SZSE:300747) subject company.

title: Supercritical CO2: Powering the Future with Efficient Waste Heat Recovery
claim_supported: John Crane's dry gas seals (Aura 220, Type 28XP/28EXP) were selected for the world's first Allam Cycle sCO2 power plant and for the SOLARSCO2OL sCO2 pilot (via Franco Tosi Meccanica); claims nearly three decades of CO2-sealing/carbon-capture experience.
locator: Blog body, Allam Cycle project reference and product-portfolio sections.
notes: Satisfies the binding P4 seal-major-mapping requirement with an adverse result: John Crane is already an active, multi-project sCO2 seal incumbent, not a hypothetical future entrant as the seed's own framing implied.

india_origin_audit.notes: UK-parented (Smiths Group plc) global industrial sealing company; John Crane's operating HQ is also commonly cited as US (Illinois) -- both non-India.
